meta.title: TechPulse
meta.tagline: Tech & AI news, hot takes, and who's saying what — from the builders you follow
meta.note: Built from your X Following timeline, filtered to tech/AI builders by profile. Click any story to see every take and what different people are saying.

stories.title: Claude Sonnet 5 Drops — But It Costs More Than Fable to Run
stories.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 5 as their most agentic model yet, landing in GitHub Copilot and Vercel's AI Gateway. The catch: Theo's benchmarks show it costs more to run than Opus 4.8 and even Fable, burning nearly 2x the tokens per task.

takes.name: Claude
takes.text:
Introducing Claude Sonnet 5, our most agentic Sonnet yet.

It makes plans, uses tools like browsers and terminals, and runs autonomously at a level that just a few months ago required larger and more expensive models. https://t.co/UKK8G7ww5h

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: Sonnet 5 cost MORE than Opus 4.8 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index https://t.co/fGqp02QinV

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: Oh my god, Sonnet 5 was MORE EXPENSIVE THAN FABLE to run the whole bench 💀 https://t.co/ZGNsWsVIay

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: How is this possible? Efficiency. It generates almost 2x as many tokens as Opus 4.8 per task. https://t.co/8xJOmtzk4W

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text:
📣 @AnthropicAI's Claude Sonnet 5 is now generally available and rolling out in GitHub Copilot.

Early testing for Claude Sonnet 5 showed:
• strong results across a range of coding scenarios, particularly for performance on CLI-style tasks.
• excellent prompt-cache utilization https://t.co/KTLpnOggXP

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
Claude Sonnet 5 is now on AI Gateway.

Limited promotional price of $2/M input, $10/M output available through Aug 31.

𝚖𝚘𝚍𝚎𝚕: '𝚊𝚗𝚝𝚑𝚛𝚘𝚙𝚒𝚌/𝚌𝚕𝚊𝚞𝚍𝚎-𝚜𝚘𝚗𝚗𝚎𝚝-𝟻'

https://t.co/GOu2v7Pcyp

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Claude Sonnet 5 is live on Higgsfield.

It plans and runs marketing asset pipelines on its own via Higgsfield MCP, at a level that used to need bigger, costlier models.

It also makes agentic pipelines more efficient on Higgsfield Supercomputer. https://t.co/zHaweJTOq6

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: BREAKING: the new sonnet is better than the old sonnet

takes.name: Kevin Kern
takes.text: is sonnet 5 safe enough that we're actually allowed to use it on launch day?

stories.title: Fable 5 is Back — After 18 Days in Anthropic's Doghouse
stories.summary: Anthropic's controversial Fable 5 model is returning after an 18-day ban, sending the timeline into a frenzy. Builders range from ecstatic to skeptical about what changed during its 'lockup.'

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: The Fable ban has officially been lifted! Should hopefully be live on Anthropic's APIs and Claude Code soon

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: They're making us wait another day???

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: It's been 18 days since Fable 5 was banned. Kind of insane. I genuinely thought it would be back after a few days.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
Anthropic plans to bring back Fable 5 tomorrow

https://t.co/T0pQvzk9OO

takes.name: Order
takes.text:
The government locked it down for being too dangerous. 

18 days later it’s cleared. 

Fable 5 is back, and I’m ready to build with it day one. 🚀 https://t.co/dB1H0rmMB2

takes.name: Steve Ruiz
takes.text: Fable's time in lockup will have changed it. Slow to laugh. you can never step in the same river twice.

takes.name: Micky
takes.text:
DO MY EYES DECEIVE ME????

Fable back tomorrow 😭😭😭🥹🥹

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text:
It’s about DANG time.

Time to absolutely hammer away at this…

They better accept my ID even though I’m not 18. https://t.co/EYp1WCXvGJ

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: mfs acting like fable 5 is needed when opus already does the same thing

stories.title: Devs Are Building Their Own AI Agents Instead of Waiting
stories.summary: A wave of builders shipping their own AI tooling: Kent built Kody (an agent that augments Cursor/Claude rather than replacing them), Matt Pocock shipped a /wizard CLI skill builder, and Mike Futia built an Agency Client Finder in Claude Code.

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text:
I built a better OpenClaw.

🐨 Kody is different.

Instead of trying to replace @cursor_ai, @OpenAI, @AnthropicAI, @opencode, @code, pi, etc with a probably worse harness, Kody augments it to turn it into a safer assistant that creates deterministic integrations/automations. https://t.co/SUVrwv0A4r

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Getting sick of setting up third-party services

So I built a skill for it

/wizard builds you an interactive CLI for the task you're currently doing, and takes as much work off your hands as possible

#1 is how the agent described the wizard, #2-3 is what it looks like: https://t.co/SwMnQOeqX8

takes.name: Mike Futia
takes.text:
I built an Agency Client Finder in Claude Code that finds DTC brands who need a creative agency, and writes the pitch for you 🤯

Drop in a niche → it scans the Meta Ad Library, finds brands that are spending on ads but running weak creative, and hands you a ranked prospect list https://t.co/LInmUs2BfK

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text:
I built 🐨 Kody to address all the frustrations I had with personal assistants.

One of the best parts about Kody is it augments your favorite agent and encourages it to move from one-off prompt to deterministic triggerable code run in a sandbox. https://t.co/Jh4A5FxZFD

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Next plan is to make a daily podcast of all the relevant stuff that came in to the wiki in the last 24 hours.

X, Discord, Gmail, Slack -> Food for thought while I wash up

takes.name: Riley Brown
takes.text:
You can build your own custom Jarvis, using Cursor, Claude Code or Codex, in less than 20 minutes.

Using the new GPT-Realtime-2 API...

Anything you can do on a computer can be turned into a tool that your Jarvis can use.

(Here's exactly how to do it)
 Introduction
 Step 1: https://t.co/WPx4G18nEH

stories.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Science — AI Built for Researchers
stories.summary: Anthropic unveiled Claude Science, a dedicated research app with 60+ scientific databases, code environments, and traceable artifacts. It's designed to handle every stage of the research workflow.

takes.name: Claude
takes.text:
Introducing Claude Science, a new app designed with every stage of research in mind. 

Artifacts traced to their code, environments managed on demand, and 60+ optional scientific databases that you can connect.

Available now in beta. https://t.co/HKhLknxLJO

takes.name: Claude
takes.text: More on Claude Science: https://t.co/65weJA2wRv

takes.name: François Chollet
takes.text:
Cross-agent feedback loops are incredibly effective -- for a reason. Check out what @leon2mcp and team at @Bloome_im are building in this space: https://t.co/a9pCRb2ELP

Bloome lets you pull Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, and human teammates into a single shared workspace. The best

takes.name: Steve Ruiz
takes.text: @claudeai https://t.co/HFB0Zmsnev

stories.title: Vercel Launches Agent in Public Beta, Teams Up with Shopify
stories.summary: Vercel dropped its new Agent in Public Beta — handling chat, investigations, plans, and pull requests. CEO Guillermo Rauch also announced a partnership with Shopify to push the agentic web forward.

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
The new Vercel Agent is in Public Beta.

▪︎ Chat
▪︎ Investigations
▪︎ Plans & approvals
▪︎ Pull requests
▪︎ Read-only by default

Details in the changelog ↓ https://t.co/mB5LSKEJcT

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
So great to get to work with @tobi and the wonderful @shopify team.

Excited to push the agentic web forward.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
At dinner, tech executive is relaying his company’s @vercel feedback, and then his 12-year-old son’s @vercel feedback 😁 

Vercel is for everyone.

takes.name: Vercel Developers

stories.title: Higgsfield Shows What Gemini Omni Flash Can Really Do in Video
stories.summary: Higgsfield AI dropped a string of demos showing Gemini Omni Flash doing AI VFX, White House documentaries, and editing — plus launched Nano Banana 2 Lite at 4s per image for $0.034/1K. Hidden capabilities in Gemini are only now being uncovered.

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
There are many hidden capabilities in Gemini Omni Flash.

We have yet to discover them all. https://t.co/3vohXFHHi3

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
A full White House documentary, made entirely on Higgsfield.

Generated in 5 clips with Gemini Omni Flash, then trimmed and merged into one film.

World knowledge with text that holds across every frame lets you scale faceless educational content. https://t.co/5MPgcMJMI9

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
AI VFX.

Generated with Gemini Omni Flash on Higgsfield. https://t.co/UPvbC06CRm

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Nano Banana 2 Lite is live on Higgsfield.

As good as Nano Banana 2, but far faster and cheaper. Under 4 seconds per image, $0.034 per 1K image.

The most efficient image model for scaling agentic pipelines, through Higgsfield MCP. https://t.co/tquc4HR9xn

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text: Editing with Gemini Omni Flash. https://t.co/KvSN41M5KJ

stories.title: Node.js Finally Gets the Web Worker API Nobody Knew They Were Waiting For
stories.summary: Colin McDonnell's Nub adds the Web Worker API to Node.js — closing the #1 most upvoted issue — bringing Node into parity with Bun and Cloudflare Workers via Node worker_threads.

takes.name: colinhacks
takes.text:
Nub adds the Web Worker API in Node.js (finally!)

✅ closes the #1 most upvoted issue on Node.js ✨
✅ brings Nub/Node into parity with Bun and Cloudflare Workers
✅ powered by Node worker_threads https://t.co/Sl8WLvt120

stories.title: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lands Bridgewater as First Named Partner
stories.summary: Ex-OpenAI CTO Mira Murati revealed Bridgewater Associates partnered with her new company Thinking Machines to fine-tune a model helping analysts focus on what matters — expert knowledge improving AI that empowers experts back.

takes.name: Mira Murati
takes.text:
Bridgewater used their unique financial knowledge and partnered with us on @tinkerapi to fine-tune a model that helps their analysts focus on what's important. Experts improving AI that empowers experts.
https://t.co/6RJITMG2BJ

stories.title: Tim Sweeney: AI is Forcing an Entirely New Computing Architecture
stories.summary: Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney says AI's massive memory-bandwidth and compute needs are breaking us out of a hardware 'innovation doldrums' and forcing entirely new architectures — a rare optimistic note from the gaming world.

takes.name: Tim Sweeney
takes.text: It's exciting to watch new computing architectures emerge from the high-memory-size, high-memory-bandwidth, high-compute, high-communication needs of AI. We've been in somewhat in the innovation doldrums with commodity PC architectures eating everything.

stories.title: TypeScript Creator: 12 Years of Issues and Decisions Still Searchable
stories.summary: GitHub highlighted how building in the open compounds over time — Anders Hejlsberg on 12 years of TypeScript decisions being searchable and building on each other, nothing lost.

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text:
The best thing about building in the open? Nothing gets lost.

Anders Hejlsberg, creator of TypeScript and C#, on 12 years of issues and decisions, all searchable. "It didn't just disappear into some email I can't find anymore."

Take a trip down memory lane and tell us: what was https://t.co/VdBP29LBvf

stories.title: Flutter Makes Its Case for the AI Era: Write Once, Save Tokens
stories.summary: The Flutter team argues a single codebase is a natural fit for agentic AI workflows: write logic once, Dart's strong typing guides LLMs better, and Hot Reload lets you validate instantly.

takes.name: Flutter
takes.text:
Why use Flutter in the AI era? Building with a single codebase gives your agentic workflows a massive advantage:
💰 Write once, save tokens
🎯 Dart’s strong typing guides LLMs
⚡️ Validate with Hot Reload

Learn best practices for AI-driven development: https://t.co/nqgLiZju0k https://t.co/TTUD2j4qkO

hotTakes.name: Can Vardar
hotTakes.text: linkedin is where llms go to socialize with each other

hotTakes.name: Codie Sanchez
hotTakes.text:
The worst thing AI did was make lazy people think they're productive.

The whole goal is to produce better things, not more.

hotTakes.name: dax
hotTakes.text: i have a difficult merge conflict so im just gonna wait till fable is back tomorrow

hotTakes.name: trash
hotTakes.text: i think it’s about time i over engineer another useless app.

hotTakes.name: Can Vardar
hotTakes.text:
benchmarks don’t work anymore

we’re not even falling for it

hotTakes.name: Riley Brown
hotTakes.text: Prediction: July will belong to DeepSeek.

hotTakes.name: Theo - t3.gg
hotTakes.text: Filmed a video about why OpenAI models are so efficient. With Sonnet 5's insane inefficiencies, feels like a good time to post it :) https://t.co/WSF4Dkboxr

hotTakes.name: dax
hotTakes.text:
when you put a mirror up to another mirror and there's infinite reflection

can we somehow harness that for energy
